AE.label: Reconsider travel
AE.summary: Reconsider travel due to regional conflict and missile/drone risk plus major flight disruption — a sharp, recent change from the UAE's normal baseline.
considerations: Elevated to Level 3 in March 2026 after US-Iran hostilities began (ordered departure of non-emergency US government staff).
The practical traveler impact is airspace/flight disruption and the volatile regional situation rather than routine local crime.
Strict local laws apply: zero tolerance for drugs (including residual amounts and CBD), public-conduct and social-media rules, photography restrictions, and Ramadan daytime-fasting norms.
Status hinges on the US-Iran situation and is fast-moving — re-verify before any booking.
AE.medical: Bottled water commonly preferred; extreme summer heat (45C+) carries serious heat-illness risk at midday.
AE.source: US State Dept L3 / UK FCDO, Mar 2026

AU.label: Exercise normal precautions
AU.summary: Australia is very safe for travelers; the main risks are natural and environmental, not crime.
considerations: Bushfire season (Nov-Mar) and tropical cyclones in the north; flash flooding.
Strong ocean rips, marine stingers and saltwater crocodiles in northern waters.
Intense sun and heat; vast outback driving distances.
AU.medical: High-quality care; tap water safe; evacuation insurance wise for remote/outback travel.

BS.label: Exercise increased caution
BS.summary: Exercise increased caution due to crime, concentrated in parts of Nassau and Freeport.
considerations: Armed robbery and burglary occur mostly in New Providence (Nassau) and Grand Bahama (Freeport).
Unregulated watercraft and jet-ski operators (assaults reported).
Swim hazards: never swim alone; occasional shark incidents.
Firearms and even a single stray round of ammunition are illegal — check bags before flying.

CA.label: Exercise normal precautions
CA.summary: Canada is very safe; ordinary precautions apply.
considerations: Petty theft and vehicle break-ins in tourist areas.
Winter snow and ice (some provinces require snow tires); remote Arctic areas have slow emergency response.
Strict firearm-declaration rules at the border.

CH.label: Exercise normal precautions
CH.summary: Switzerland is very safe; ordinary precautions apply.
considerations: Year-round alpine hazards: avalanche, sudden weather changes, unmarked terrain.
Pickpocketing at tourist sites and on public transport.
Medical care is excellent but expensive — carry insurance with evacuation cover.
CH.medical: Tap water is among the cleanest in Europe; healthcare is not free.

CL.label: Exercise increased caution
CL.summary: Exercise increased caution due to rising crime and periodic large demonstrations.
considerations: Street crime (muggings, pickpocketing) is common and violent crime is increasing in cities.
Large protests in Santiago and elsewhere arise with little notice and disrupt transport.
Secure valuables even in locked vehicles.
CL.medical: A low-level Hantavirus (Andes virus) health notice exists, but risk to typical visitors is very low.

CO.label: Reconsider travel
CO.summary: Reconsider travel due to crime, terrorism, kidnapping and civil unrest, with several regions off-limits entirely.
considerations: Several departments are Level 4 "Do Not Travel": Arauca, Norte de Santander, parts of Cauca (except Popayan) and Valle del Cauca (except Cali), and within ~10 km of the Venezuela border.
Violent crime and express kidnapping occur nationwide.
Terrorist attacks target transit and government sites; protests can turn violent.
Active volcanoes and landslides in some regions.
CO.medical: Yellow-fever vaccination is recommended for lower-altitude/jungle regions (verify per region).
CO.source: US State Dept L3 / UK FCDO, Mar 2026

DE.label: Exercise increased caution
DE.summary: Exercise increased caution due to terrorism risk.
considerations: Authorities assess attacks as likely (markets, transit, places of worship, events); recent incidents are on record.
Standard urban theft and pickpocketing at airports, stations and crowded venues.

ES.label: Exercise increased caution
ES.summary: Exercise increased caution due to terrorism risk and notably high tourist-area street crime.
considerations: Authorities assess terrorist attacks as likely.
Widespread pickpocketing and car break-ins in tourist areas, airports and stations (distraction-team tactics).
Demonstrations occasionally turn violent.
A documented sexual-assault risk for younger travelers, often alcohol- or drug-involved.

FR.label: Exercise increased caution
FR.summary: Exercise increased caution due to terrorism and persistent petty crime in tourist cities.
considerations: Authorities assess terrorist attacks as likely (tourist sites, transit, gatherings).
Frequent pickpocketing and phone theft on the Paris Metro/RER and at stations.
Strikes and demonstrations are common and occasionally disruptive.

GR.label: Exercise normal precautions
GR.summary: Greece is generally safe; ordinary precautions apply.
considerations: Pickpocketing in tourist hotspots (Athens metro, ferry terminals).
Occasional large demonstrations that disrupt transport.
Wildfire season Apr-Oct and severe summer heat.
GR.medical: Tap water is generally safe on the mainland; bottled is advised on some smaller islands; mind summer heat.

ID.label: Exercise increased caution
ID.summary: Exercise increased caution due to terrorism and natural hazards; parts of Papua are off-limits.
considerations: Central Papua and Highland Papua are Level 4 "Do Not Travel" (conflict, armed separatists, kidnapping).
High seismic and volcanic activity (earthquakes, tsunamis, eruptions).
Ongoing terrorism plotting; variable road and ferry safety.
ID.medical: Tap water is not safe — drink bottled/boiled; dengue is endemic, malaria in Papua/eastern/rural areas, rabies present (incl. Bali); Hep A/B and typhoid recommended.
ID.source: US State Dept L2 / UK FCDO, Apr 2025

IS.label: Exercise normal precautions
IS.summary: Iceland is very safe; the real risks are environmental, not crime.
considerations: Rapidly changing, severe weather is the top hazard.
Ongoing Reykjanes Peninsula volcanic activity can affect the Grindavik area and occasionally airport access.
Glaciers, river crossings and sneaker waves require care.
IS.medical: Excellent tap water; cold and exposure risk in the highlands.

IT.label: Exercise increased caution
IT.summary: Exercise increased caution due to terrorism risk and heavy tourist-area petty crime.
considerations: Possible terrorist attacks at tourist, transit and religious sites.
Prevalent pickpocketing and bag-snatching on transit and at major attractions.
Demonstrations can turn disruptive.
Seismic activity and active southern volcanoes (Stromboli, Vesuvius/Campi Flegrei).

JO.label: Reconsider travel
JO.summary: Reconsider travel due to terrorism and regional conflict; several border zones are off-limits.
considerations: Level 4 "Do Not Travel" zones include the Syria/Iraq border areas, designated refugee camps, Rusayfah, and the Baqa'a area of Ayn Basha.
Conflict-driven drone/missile risk region-wide since Feb 2026.
The core Amman-Petra-Wadi Rum-Dead Sea circuit sits away from the Level 4 zones, but the country-wide Level 3 is volatile.
Modest dress and customs sensitivity at religious sites.
JO.medical: Drink bottled water; extreme desert heat and sun exposure at Petra/Wadi Rum — hydrate and protect from sun.
JO.source: US State Dept L3 / UK FCDO, May 2026

JP.label: Exercise normal precautions
JP.summary: Japan is very safe; ordinary precautions apply.
considerations: Significant natural-hazard exposure: earthquakes, tsunamis, typhoons, heavy northern winter snow.
Very strict drug laws — some common US prescription medicines are banned.
Take care with spiked-drink incidents in major nightlife districts.
JP.medical: Excellent care; tap water safe; some US prescription medicines are illegal — check before travel and carry enough supply.

KE.label: Exercise increased caution
KE.summary: Exercise increased caution; mainstream safari areas are Level 2, but several border and coastal zones are off-limits.
considerations: Level 4 "Do Not Travel" zones: Somalia-border counties (Mandera, Wajir, Garissa), parts of the coast (Lamu County, Tana River, Kilifi north of Malindi) and northern border areas; Level 3 for Nairobi's Eastleigh and Kibera.
Violent crime and carjacking, including in Nairobi.
Poor road safety — avoid night driving.
Maasai Mara, Amboseli and Tsavo sit in the Level 2 zone.
KE.medical: Yellow-fever vaccination recommended (certificate required if arriving from a risk country); malaria prophylaxis strongly advised for safari areas; bottled/treated water; carry medevac insurance.
KE.source: US State Dept L2 / UK FCDO, Mar 2025

KH.label: Exercise increased caution
KH.summary: Exercise increased caution; most of the country is fine, but the Thailand border is off-limits and landmines persist in some rural areas.
considerations: Level 4 "Do Not Travel" within ~50 km of the Cambodia-Thailand border (2025 conflict; a ceasefire was signed Dec 2025 but crossings remain suspended and landmine/UXO risk persists).
Street crime in Phnom Penh (phone and bag snatching), with occasional violent incidents.
Unexploded ordnance and landmines in remote/forested areas (Battambang, Banteay Meanchey, Pursat, Pailin) — use local guides.
Poor road safety.
KH.medical: Tap water is not safe — drink bottled/boiled; dengue is endemic and malaria occurs in rural/forest/border areas; serious cases evacuate to Bangkok — carry evacuation insurance.
KH.source: US State Dept L2 / UK FCDO, Jul 2025

KR.label: Exercise normal precautions
KR.summary: South Korea (Republic of Korea) is very safe; ordinary precautions apply — this is not North Korea, which is Do Not Travel.
considerations: Periodic North Korean missile tests and routine civil-defense drills are background context, not an elevated risk.
Large demonstrations can arise quickly; foreigners are barred from political activity by law.
Typhoon season in summer/fall; very low crime.
KR.medical: Excellent care; tap water generally safe; spring fine-dust/air-quality advisories.

LC.label: Exercise normal precautions
LC.summary: Saint Lucia is island-wide Level 1, but one specific resort has a current US Embassy security alert.
considerations: A Feb 2026 US Embassy alert specifically warns against staying at the Ladera Resort near Soufriere (crime risk and limited emergency response) — the island-wide level remains Level 1.
Most of the island remains low-risk.
Usual precautions: stay aware, do not resist robbery, carry a passport copy.
LC.source: US State Dept L1, Aug 2024 + US Embassy Bridgetown alert, Feb 2026

NA.label: Exercise increased caution
NA.summary: Exercise increased caution due to crime; no regional carve-outs.
considerations: Opportunistic and property crime, especially in Windhoek.
The dominant real risk is self-drive gravel-road safety (rollovers, wildlife, fatigue) across vast distances.
Very limited medical and emergency services in remote areas.
NA.medical: Yellow fever required only if arriving from a risk country; seasonal malaria in the north (Zambezi/Caprivi, Kavango, Etosha fringe); medevac insurance strongly advised.

NL.label: Exercise increased caution
NL.summary: Exercise increased caution due to terrorism risk.
considerations: Authorities assess continued attack plotting (tourist sites, transit, gatherings).
Standard urban pickpocketing in crowded Amsterdam tourist areas and on transit.

NO.label: Exercise normal precautions
NO.summary: Norway is among the safest destinations; the risks are environmental.
considerations: Very low crime.
Arctic and mountain weather, winter avalanche zones, and fjord/hiking exposure are the main hazards.
Svalbard requires special preparation (polar-bear risk outside settlements).
NO.medical: Excellent tap water; cold-exposure and winter-driving risk.

NZ.label: Exercise normal precautions
NZ.summary: New Zealand is very safe; ordinary precautions apply.
considerations: Driving on the left on narrow rural roads is a leading cause of visitor injury.
Earthquakes, volcanic activity and fast-changing alpine/coastal weather.
Vehicle and hostel theft in tourist areas; strict biosecurity declarations.
NZ.medical: Care is not free — carry insurance with evacuation cover; tap water safe.

PE.label: Exercise increased caution
PE.summary: Exercise increased caution; the tourist circuit is Level 2, but two remote regions are off-limits.
considerations: Level 4 "Do Not Travel" zones: the VRAEM (Apurimac/Ene/Mantaro valley) and the Colombia-border area of Loreto.
Petty theft, carjackings and muggings occur even in daylight.
Demonstrations can block roads and rail without warning.
Cusco, the Sacred Valley, the Inca Trail and Machu Picchu are NOT in the restricted zones.
PE.medical: Altitude is the key concern — Cusco (~3,400 m) and the Sacred Valley/Inca Trail carry real altitude-sickness risk; acclimatize first. Yellow-fever vaccination recommended for the Amazon/jungle.
PE.source: US State Dept L2 / UK FCDO, May 2025

PF.label: Exercise normal precautions
PF.summary: French Polynesia (Tahiti, Bora Bora, the Society Islands) is low-risk; ordinary precautions apply. It has its own US advisory, separate from mainland France.
considerations: Generally low crime, with occasional petty theft.
Cyclone season Nov-Apr.
Outer-atoll remoteness means limited services and slower emergency response.
PF.medical: Good care on Tahiti, limited on outer atolls; mosquito-borne illness (e.g. dengue) present; evacuation insurance advised.

PT.label: Exercise normal precautions
PT.summary: Portugal is generally safe; ordinary precautions apply.
considerations: Pickpocketing and bag-snatching at popular sites and on transit; car break-ins, especially of rentals.
Summer wildfire risk on the mainland.
Note local laws: cannabis/CBD and pepper spray are illegal.

RW.label: Reconsider travel
RW.summary: Reconsider travel due to a regional Ebola health emergency and DRC-border conflict spillover.
considerations: Driven by a regional Ebola public-health emergency (declared May 2026) plus eastern-DRC conflict.
Level 4 "Do Not Travel" within ~10 km of the DRC border (incl. parts of Rusizi, Lake Kivu islands, Kamembe airport).
Reduced US emergency-consular capacity; Kigali itself is normally very safe.
Gorilla-trekking areas (Volcanoes NP) are near the northwest border — check status before booking.
RW.medical: Active regional Ebola outbreak with heightened screening; yellow fever required if arriving from a transmission country; malaria prophylaxis; bottled/treated water; medevac insurance essential.
RW.source: US State Dept L3 / UK FCDO / WHO Ebola emergency, Jun 2026

SA.label: Reconsider travel
SA.summary: Reconsider travel due to regional conflict, terrorism and missile/drone risk; some areas are off-limits.
considerations: Level 4 "Do Not Travel" zones: the Yemen border region (within ~20 mi) and Qatif (Eastern Province).
Conflict-driven drone/missile risk and falling intercept debris since Feb 2026.
Exit bans can block departure during legal, commercial or family disputes.
Strict laws on social-media posts, drugs, alcohol (prohibited), dress/behavior, and photography of government/military sites.
SA.medical: Drink bottled water; extreme summer heat (45-50C); Hajj/Umrah pilgrims must meet Saudi vaccination requirements (e.g. meningococcal ACWY) — verify current rules.
SA.source: US State Dept L3 / UK FCDO, Mar 2026

TC.label: Exercise increased caution
TC.summary: Exercise increased caution due to crime, mostly on Providenciales. It has its own US advisory, not folded under the UK.
considerations: Most crime is on Providenciales; a mix of opportunistic theft and occasional violent crime.
Police have limited investigative resources.
Firearms and even a single stray round of ammunition are illegal and strictly enforced (including on departure) — inspect all bags before flying.

TH.label: Exercise increased caution
TH.summary: Exercise increased caution; most of the country is fine, but the deep south and the Cambodia border are off-limits or sensitive.
considerations: Level 4 "Do Not Travel" within ~50 km of the Cambodia border (2025 conflict; a ceasefire was signed Dec 2025 but crossings remain suspended and landmine/UXO risk persists).
Deep-south insurgency provinces (Yala, Pattani, Narathiwat) are under increased caution / state of emergency.
Poor road safety (very high motorcycle fatality rate); monsoon flooding.
TH.medical: Tap water is not safe — bottled/boiled; dengue endemic year-round, malaria in border/forest areas, rabies present; Hep A/B and typhoid recommended; excellent private hospitals in Bangkok.
TH.source: US State Dept L2 / UK FCDO, Jul 2025

TZ.label: Reconsider travel
TZ.summary: Reconsider travel due to post-election unrest, crime and terrorism; the northern safari circuit is far from the worst areas but the country-wide level is elevated.
considerations: Elevated to Level 3 after the Oct 2025 election (protests, curfews).
Terrorist-violence risk is highest in the southern Mtwara region near the Mozambique/Cabo Delgado border (avoid within ~20 km of that border).
Violent crime with limited police response.
Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire and Kilimanjaro are far from Mtwara.
TZ.medical: Yellow fever required if arriving from a risk country (Zanzibar enforces strictly); high malaria risk across the mainland and Zanzibar — prophylaxis strongly advised; Kilimanjaro's main hazard is altitude sickness; bottled/treated water; medevac insurance.
TZ.source: US State Dept L3 / UK FCDO, Oct 2025

ZA.label: Exercise increased caution
ZA.summary: Exercise increased caution due to high violent-crime rates.
considerations: Armed robbery, carjacking and smash-and-grab at intersections.
US-government staff need special clearance for many Cape Town informal settlements (a de facto elevated zone).
Periodic protests and power outages affecting security systems; use caution with e-hailing/taxis.
Kruger and private reserves are generally well-managed.
ZA.medical: Malaria risk in the northeast low-veld (Kruger, Limpopo, Mpumalanga, far-north KwaZulu-Natal) — prophylaxis advised there; Cape Town, the Garden Route and the Winelands are malaria-free; yellow fever required only if arriving from a risk country; tap water generally safe in major cities.
